sections.paragraph:
γέννησις, εως, ἡ (s. γένεσις; Eur., Pla.; SIG 1109, 130; PLond V, 1731, 10 [VI a.d.]; PGM 13, 981; Eccl 7:1 v.l. ἡμέρα γεννήσεως; TestSol C 10:4; Just., D. 100, 3; ἐκ παρθένου γ. τοῦ θεοῦ Orig., C. Cels. 6, 73, 2) birth Mt 1:18 v.l.; Lk 1:14 v.l.; 1J 5:18 v.l.; IMg 11. Variant in book title of GJs, s. deStrycker 211.—M-M. DELG s.v. γίγνομαι p. 222.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 194
